schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: tation frequency $\Omega$ is: \begin{equation} H_m(\Omega, t) = \exp\left[i\left(\omega_m t + \Phi_{\text{Berry}}^{(m)} + \Phi_{\text{Sagnac}}^{(m)}(\Omega)\right)\right] \end{equation} where: \begin{align} \omega_m &= c\sqrt{k_z^2 + (m/R)^2} + \frac{2m\Omega}{n}\\ \Phi_{\text{Berry}}^{(m)} &= \frac{2\ln\varphi}{\pi} m t\\ \Phi_{\text{Sagnac}}^{(m)}(\Omega) &= \frac{4\pi\Omega R^2}{\lambda_0 c} m \end{align} with $k_z = 2\pi/P$ the axial wavevector, $n$ the refractive index, $\varphi$ the golden ratio, and $\lambda_0$ the central wavelength. \end{proposition} The Berry phase contribution $\Phi_{\text{Berry}}^{(m)}$ arises from the geometric properties of helical paths in momentum space, while the Sagnac phase $\Phi_{\text{Sagnac}}^{(m)}(\Omega)$ results from the rotation of the reference frame. Both effects scale linearly with the OAM quantum number $m$, leading to mode-dependent phase accumulation that can be exploited for multiplexing and demultiplexing operations. \subsection{Gerchberg-Saxton Iterative Phase Retrieval} Converting a target intensity distribution into the required input phase pattern co
theory.technical_interpretation: Relation: Φ_Berry^(m) is a symbolic expression, written Φ_Berry^(m). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from m. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
